fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Ohio Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer dietitian license to ohio, dietitian compact ohio, ohio dietitian endorsement application

board.name: State Medical Board of Ohio
board.mailing_address:
Ohio Board of Dietetics
77 South High Street, 16th Floor
Columbus, OH 43215-6108

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Standard independent credential regulated by the State Medical Board of Ohio for the practice of dietetics.

license_types.title: Limited Permit
license_types.description: A permit for individuals who have met academic and pre-professional requirements and are awaiting examination. Costs $68.50 including transaction fees.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility & Pass CDR Exam
steps.description: Ensure you meet the education and supervised practice requirements and have successfully passed the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination, as these are prerequisites for licensure in Ohio.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Ohio Account
steps.description: Navigate to the eLicense Ohio portal at elicense.ohio.gov and register for a new account by providing your personal information to establish your login credentials.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your eLicense Ohio account, select the 'Dietitian Initial License' application, and accurately complete all required sections of the application form.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Electronically upload or arrange for official transcripts, verification of supervised practice completion, and your CDR exam scores to be sent directly to the Ohio Board of Dietetics through the eLicense Ohio system or via official institutional channels.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable $100.00 application fee using a credit card or electronic check directly within the eLicense Ohio portal to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: Await review by the Ohio Board of Dietetics; once all submitted requirements are verified and approved, your license will be issued and viewable on the eLicense Ohio portal.

documents.detail: Directly submitted transcripts denoting the awarding of your degree.
Proof of active status with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).
FBI and Ohio BCI background check reports transmitted directly from the fingerprinting agency to the Board.

provisional_text: Ohio offers a Limited Permit for prospective licensees who have finished their education and internship hours but are waiting to take the CDR exam.
reciprocity_text: Under Ohio's Universal Recognition path, out-of-state dietitians can apply for reciprocity if they have held a substantially similar, active, and good-standing license in another jurisdiction for at least one year immediately preceding the application.

faqs.question: How much does the Ohio Licensed Dietitian application cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license costs $228.50 ($225 base fee plus a $3.50 transaction fee levied by the eLicense system).

faqs.question: Which Ohio agency regulates dietitians?
faqs.answer: Unlike many states with specialized dietetics boards, dietitians in Ohio are licensed and regulated by the State Medical Board of Ohio.

faqs.question: What continuing education is required in Ohio?
faqs.answer: You must maintain the CDR's requirement of 75 hours per five-year cycle. Additionally, Ohio dictates that 1 hour of CE during every two-year state renewal cycle must specifically cover ethics, laws, and rules governing practice in Ohio.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Ohio has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Ohio?
Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Under Ohio's Universal Recognition path, out-of-state dietitians can apply for reciprocity if they have held a substantially similar, active, and good-standing license in another jurisdiction for at least one year immediately preceding the application.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Ohio
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian license in Ohio, applicants must use the eLicense Ohio portal for initial application, documentation submission, and fee payment.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to elicense.ohio.gov, click 'Register a New Account', and follow the prompts to create your user ID and password. Once registered, log in to begin your application.
